meta_title: For Founders: community building to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to community building and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Community Building to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn how to leverage community building to identify high-revenue SaaS opportunities using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for ideas in a vacuum. The most profitable SaaS products are born from community pain points. By building a community-first feedback loop, you validate demand before writing a single line of production code. Use Antigravity to prototype and Claude Code to iterate rapidly based on real user feedback.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. You have the technical stack, the compute credits, and the ambition, but the ‘what’ remains elusive. You’re not suffering from a lack of skill; you’re suffering from a lack of signal. Most founders build in silos, crafting elegant solutions for problems that don’t exist, only to launch into a deafening silence.

True innovation isn't a bolt of lightning—it’s an echo. By shifting your focus from ‘building a product’ to ‘building a community,’ you transform your development process from a guessing game into a data-driven mission. This is how you stop chasing ideas and start capturing revenue.

steps.title: Identify the High-Value Niche
steps.description: Engage with specific subreddits or Discord servers to find recurring 'How do I...' questions. Look for manual processes that users are currently hacking together with spreadsheets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided dataset of community complaints and identify three recurring pain points that can be solved with a SaaS tool. Rank them by monetization potential and technical feasibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research current top 50 trending discussions in the [Niche] community and summarize the most common bottlenecks.'

steps.title: Build the Minimal Landing Page
steps.description: Create a landing page that captures emails based on the identified pain point. You aren't selling software yet; you're selling a solution to their specific problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-converting landing page structure using Tailwind CSS and Next.js. Focus on the 'Problem-Agitation-Solution' framework to maximize email signups.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a Next.js landing page with a hero section, email capture form, and feature grid based on the identified pain point.'

steps.title: Develop the 'Concierge' MVP
steps.description: Don't automate everything immediately. Build a thin wrapper that allows you to manually provide the service behind the scenes while the UI looks fully functional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a schema for a database to track user requests and manual fulfillment status. Include API endpoints for basic user authentication and request submission.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the backend boilerplate for a Supabase-powered CRUD application to manage user request queues.'

steps.title: Implement Feedback Loops
steps.description: Integrate a feedback widget directly into the MVP. Every time a user interacts with the tool, prompt them for a specific improvement suggestion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a lightweight React component for a feedback modal that sends user input directly to a Slack webhook or Discord channel.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Add a feedback component to the main dashboard that captures user sentiment and saves it to the database.'

steps.title: Pivot to Productized Service
steps.description: Use the data collected from your community to prioritize your roadmap. Automate the most requested manual tasks using your AI stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the collected feedback logs and extract the top three features that users are willing to pay for. Propose a technical implementation plan for these features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Review the feedback database and generate a prioritized feature roadmap for the next 2 weeks of development.'

pro_tips: Never build a feature until at least 5 community members ask for it independently.
Use Discord as your 'Product Requirement Document' (PRD) by pinning high-value feature requests.
Charge $1 for your MVP—it filters out noise and proves actual willingness to pay.
Automate your community engagement using Claude Code to summarize daily conversations into actionable dev tasks.
Build in public by sharing screenshots of your Claude Code terminal sessions to build trust and authority.
content.next_steps: Once you have 50+ active users and consistent feedback, begin the process of formalizing your pricing tiers and scaling your backend infrastructure.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea to strike—create it through the power of community feedback and rapid iteration. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
